title: Git worktree manager with session tracking and PR creation capabilities
purpose: Manages Git worktrees for collaborative sessions, handles creation, cleanup, and Git operations like committing and pushing changes with PR creation.
module_context: Exports a WorktreeManager class that manages Git worktrees for sessions, tracks them in JSON metadata, and provides methods for Git operations including commit/push/PR creation.

steps.content: Check if a worktree is already being ensured for this session.
IF a pending ensure exists, return the existing promise.
ELSE begin a new ensure process and store the promise.
Once the ensure completes, remove the stored promise.
Return the result of the ensure process.
Attempt to read a cached worktree record for this session.
IF a cached record exists and the directory is valid, return it.
ELSE delete the stale record and proceed to create a new worktree.
Check if the project root is a Git repository.
IF not a Git repository, return a non-git fallback.
Detect the base branch for the worktree.
Generate a branch name and path for the new worktree.
Handle path collisions by appending a random suffix.
Ensure the base directory exists for worktrees.
Attempt to create the worktree with Git.
IF the first attempt fails, clean up and retry once.
IF the retry also fails, throw an error with details.
Write the new worktree record to persistent storage.
Return the newly created worktree information.
Read the worktree record for this session.
IF no record exists, return immediately.
Attempt to remove the worktree directory from Git.
IF the removal fails, check if the error is benign.
IF the error is not benign, attempt to remove the directory using filesystem operations.
Delete the persistent record for this session.
Read the worktree record for this session.
IF no record exists, return false.
Check if the worktree directory exists.
IF the directory does not exist, return false.
Run Git status to check for uncommitted changes.
IF the Git command fails, return false.
Return true if there are uncommitted changes.
Read the worktree record for this session.
IF no record exists, return null.
Check if the worktree directory exists.
IF the directory does not exist, return null.
Run Git symbolic-ref to get the current branch name.
IF the Git command fails, return null.
Return the branch name if it is not empty.
Get the directory path for worktree records.
Read all files in the records directory.
IF the directory does not exist, return an empty list.
For each file, check if it has a JSON extension.
Read and parse the JSON content of each file.
Validate the parsed content as a worktree info object.
Add valid worktree info objects to the output list.
Return the list of valid worktree info objects.
Read the worktree record for this session.
IF no record exists, throw an error.
Check if there are uncommitted changes or unpushed commits.
IF there are no changes to commit, throw an error.
IF there are changes, add all files to Git.
Commit the changes with the provided title and body.
Record the commit SHA if the commit is successful.
Push the changes to the remote repository.
IF GitHub CLI is available, create a pull request.
Return the result including branch, commit SHA, PR URL, and push status.
Generate the file path for the worktree record.
Read the JSON file at the generated path.
IF the file does not exist, return null.
Parse the JSON content into a worktree info object.
Return the parsed worktree info object.
Ensure the directory for worktree records exists.
Generate the file path for the worktree record.
Write the worktree info object to the JSON file.
Generate the file path for the worktree record.
Delete the JSON file at the generated path.
IF the file does not exist, ignore the error.
Run Git rev-parse to check if the project root is a Git repository.
IF the Git command succeeds, return true.
ELSE return false.
Check if the GitHub CLI is available by running its version command.
IF the CLI is not available, return false.
Check if the user is authenticated with GitHub CLI.
IF the user is not authenticated, return false.
ELSE return true.
Run Git symbolic-ref to get the current branch name.
IF the command succeeds and returns a branch name, return it.
ELSE run Git rev-parse to get the current commit hash.
IF the command succeeds, return the commit hash.
ELSE throw an error indicating the base branch cannot be determined.
Convert the session ID to lowercase.
Replace non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens.
Trim leading and trailing hyphens.
Limit the length to 48 characters.
IF the result is empty, return 'session'.
Get the current date and time.
Format the date as YYYYMMDD.
Format the time as HHMM.
Combine the date and time with a hyphen separator.
Attempt to access the file or directory at the given path.
IF the access succeeds, return true.
ELSE return false.

methods.name: runGit
methods.normalized_params: (cwd: string, args: string[], timeoutMs?: number, onProgress?: CommitPushPROpts['onProgress']): Promise<SpawnResult>

steps.content: Prepare the Git command with the working directory.
Call the spawn function to execute the Git command.
Set up a timeout to kill the process if it takes too long.
Read the stdout and stderr streams from the process.
Wait for the process to exit and collect the exit code.
Return the result including code, stdout, and stderr.

methods.name: runCmd
methods.normalized_params: (bin: string, cwd: string, args: string[], timeoutMs?: number, onProgress?: CommitPushPROpts['onProgress']): Promise<SpawnResult>

steps.content: Prepare the command with the working directory.
Call the spawn function to execute the command.
Set up a timeout to kill the process if it takes too long.
Read the stdout and stderr streams from the process.
Wait for the process to exit and collect the exit code.
Return the result including code, stdout, and stderr.
